info.name: Content Engine — Bulk & Delete APIs
info.description:
Focused collection covering:
1. Bulk create topic (auto section + auto cache flush)
2. Bulk create language (sections + topics + cache)
3. Delete topic (soft / hard / cascade)
4. Delete language (soft / hard)

Set variables before running:
  baseUrl  → http://localhost:3001
  adminKey → your-actual-secret-key

item.name: 🚀  Bulk Create Topic
item.description:
POST /v1/admin/bulk/topic

One call does everything:
✅ Auto-creates section if missing
✅ Saves topic + all blocks
✅ Extracts quizzes → quiz_questions table
✅ Extracts mermaid diagrams → diagrams table
✅ Flushes topic + nav + language cache

No separate section create or cache flush needed.

item.name: Insert DSA Arrays Topic — Full (All Block Types)

request.description:
Complete Java DSA Arrays topic with ALL block types:

• text — intro paragraph
• concept_cards — 6 key concepts
• diagram — mermaid Big-O chart (stored in diagrams table automatically)
• code — runnable playground with expected output
• note — tip callout
• warning — caveat callout
• quiz x3 — extracted into quiz_questions table automatically

The 'dsa' section is created automatically under Java if it doesn't exist.
Cache is flushed automatically — no extra calls needed.

Expected response:
{
  "ok": true,
  "path": "java.dsa.arrays",
  "id": "uuid...",
  "blocks": 9,
  "sectionCreated": "dsa",
  "cacheFlushes": ["topic:java.dsa.arrays", "nav:java", "lang:all"]
}

Deep dive child of java.dsa.arrays.

Path java.dsa.arrays.multidimensional is depth 3 — automatically flagged as deep dive.
Parent topic java.dsa.arrays must exist before sending this.

Send AFTER the parent topic insert succeeds.

Minimal topic example with text, concept_cards, and one quiz.
Good starting template for new topics.
Section 'dsa' is auto-created if it doesn't exist.

item.name: 🌐  Bulk Create Language
item.description:
POST /v1/admin/bulk/language

One call does everything:
✅ Creates / updates language
✅ Creates all sections
✅ Auto-creates sections referenced in topics but not listed in sections[]
✅ Saves all topics sorted by depth (parents before children)
✅ Extracts all quizzes and diagrams
✅ Flushes entire language cache

No other API calls needed.

item.name: Insert Python Language — Full (Language + Sections + 2 Topics)

request.description:
Inserts a complete Python language in one call:

• Language: python, color #3572A5
• Sections: core-language, advanced
• Topics:
  - python.basics (text, concept_cards, code, quiz)
  - python.variables (text, concept_cards, code, quiz)

All quizzes extracted to quiz_questions table.
Full cache flushed at the end.

Expected response:
{
  "ok": true,
  "language": { "slug": "python", "id": "uuid..." },
  "sectionsCreated": ["core-language", "advanced"],
  "topics": ["python.basics", "python.variables"],
  "errors": [],
  "cacheFlushes": ["all-topics", "nav:python", "lang:all"]
}

item.name: Insert Language + Sections Only (No Topics Yet)

request.description:
Creates just the language and its sections without topics.
Topics can be added later via POST /v1/admin/bulk/topic.

Useful when you want to set up structure first and fill topics gradually.

item.name: 🗑️  Delete Topic
item.description:
DELETE /v1/admin/delete/topic/:path?mode=soft|hard|cascade

Three modes:
• soft    → hides topic (is_published=false). Reversible.
• hard    → permanently deletes topic + blocks. Children kept.
• cascade → permanently deletes topic + ALL children at any depth.

Default mode is soft — safe to call without ?mode param.
Cache is flushed automatically in all modes.

query.description: hard = permanent delete of this topic only

request.description:
Permanently deletes the topic row and all its blocks.
Children (deep dive sub-topics) are NOT deleted — they become orphans.

⚠️ This cannot be undone.

Use 'cascade' mode instead if you also want children deleted.

Expected response:
{
  "ok": true,
  "mode": "hard",
  "deleted": "java.dsa.arrays",
  "note": "Topic and its blocks permanently deleted. Children are NOT deleted."
}

item.name: Cascade Delete Topic (Permanent — Topic + ALL Children)

query.description: cascade = permanent delete of topic + all children at any depth

request.description:
Permanently deletes the topic AND every child at any depth.

Example: deleting java.dsa.arrays with cascade also deletes:
  java.dsa.arrays.multidimensional
  java.dsa.arrays.sorting
  java.dsa.arrays.searching
  ... and any deeper children

⚠️ This cannot be undone.

Expected response:
{
  "ok": true,
  "mode": "cascade",
  "deleted": ["java.dsa.arrays", "java.dsa.arrays.multidimensional"],
  "count": 2,
  "note": "2 topic(s) and all their blocks permanently deleted."
}

item.name: 🌐🗑️  Delete Language
item.description:
DELETE /v1/admin/delete/language/:slug?mode=soft|hard

Two modes:
• soft → sets is_active=false. Hidden from all APIs. Reversible.
• hard → permanently deletes language + ALL sections + ALL topics + ALL blocks.

Default mode is soft.
Cache is flushed automatically in both modes.

request.description:
Sets is_active = false.
Language disappears from the learner app language selector.
All topics and sections are preserved.

Restore with:
  PUT /v1/admin/languages/{{langSlug}}
  Body: { "isActive": true }

Cache flushed automatically.

Expected response:
{
  "ok": true,
  "mode": "soft",
  "deactivated": "python",
  "name": "Python",
  "note": "Restore with PUT /admin/languages/:slug { isActive: true }"
}

query.description: hard = permanent wipe of entire language

request.description:
⚠️ PERMANENT FULL WIPE — CANNOT BE UNDONE

Deletes everything for this language:
  • Language row
  • All sections
  • All topics
  • All topic blocks

The database FK cascade handles cleanup automatically.

Expected response:
{
  "ok": true,
  "mode": "hard",
  "deleted": "python",
  "name": "Python",
  "summary": {
    "sectionsDeleted": 2,
    "topicsDeleted": 5,
    "blocksDeleted": 38
  },
  "note": "Language, all sections, all topics, and all blocks permanently deleted."
}
